title: podman image scp never enter podman user NS
body:
#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Podman image scp should never enter the Podman UserNS unless it needs to. This allows for
a sudo exec.Command to transfer images to and from rootful storage. If this command is run using sudo,
the simple sudo podman save/load does not work, machinectl/su is necessary here.

This modification allows for both rootful and rootless transfers, and an overall change of scp to be
more of a wrapper function for different load and save calls as well as the ssh component

Signed-off-by: cdoern <cdoern@redhat.com>

#### How to verify it

`podman image scp root@localhost::$IMAGE $USER@localhost::` (load image from root storage to user storage)

`podman image scp $USER@localhost::$IMAGE root@localhost::` (load image from user storage to root storage)

comments.body: @rhatdan @giuseppe PTAL, all of the new system tests are passing which means this is actually working. Could probably use some trimming in places but overall I think the logic here is good.

/approve
I will try this out tomorrow

What did I do wrong?
```
./bin/podman image scp docker.io/library/golang:1.17 root@localhost::docker.io/library/golang:1.17
[sudo] password for dwalsh: 
Error: docker.io/library/golang:1.17: image not known
Error: exit status 125
```

What did I do wrong? Hind only specified one ':' which is going to be normal issue for users.  Could be nicer with your help.
```
./bin/podman image scp docker.io/library/golang:1.17 root@localhost:docker.io/library/golang:1.17
Error: no image provided 
```
Which image is missing source or destination?

Nice
```
$ ./bin/podman image scp root@localhost::alpine .
Copying blob 1a058d5342cc done  
Copying config 0a97eee804 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 1a058d5342cc done  
Copying config 0a97eee804 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Loaded image(s): docker.io/library/alpine:latest
```
Not nice
```
$ ./bin/podman image scp alpine root@localhost::
Error: no image provided
```

Yikes it looks like the destination file has to preexist for this to work.
```
./bin/podman image scp alpine root@localhost::alpine1
Error: alpine1: image not known
Error: exit status 125
```
@rhatdan PTAL. I think I covered your syntax and struct changes.
@cdoern This is getting easier to understand, but I still see improvements to readability.  And handling of Users is confusion.  Pehaps the User should always be configured.

@rhatdan, I made it so that user => user transfer is possible. I also split the rootless and rootful transfer into two functions. The user => user utilizes the rootful path (but does not actually utilize the rootful aspcect) since that can login to specific users to execute the command.
@rhatdan fixed a little bug that was causing the system tests to fail. I think this looks pretty readable now, WDYT?
@cdoern looks like you're still chasing some unhappy system tests.

@TomSweeneyRedHat I think they are flakes. They were the same failure and one of them just got through. I think they were log failures?

Just rebased, too coincidental that two of them failed exactly the same on different hosts. That should fix it.
@rhatdan I took out the integration tests, they were effectively no-ops and now we have unit tests for syntax and system tests for full functionality.
If you write the line of code that says `else if ...` Think, could I do this with a switch statement.
ok @rhatdan, I think I got a lot of your cleanup and made a few nice functions. most of the parseArgs function is now switch statements.

@cevich is this one of those unfixable gitlab errors? I have rebased and vendored a few times to no avail. My only guess is that I could have moved an exec.Command into a file that this runner does not like? 

see: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner/-/blob/main/helpers/patches/issue_28732/syscall.go

this is what is failing

Edit: still failing even after moving all syscall references out of infra/abi... not sure what to do

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cdoern*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
think I got most of the redundancy @rhatdan let me know if anything else looks poorly written.

I think that fixes a lot of the syntax issues @rhatdan made it so that the arg function evaluates one at a time, and also consolidated `execSave` and `execLoad` into a single `execPodman` since they did the same thing with different commands.
@rhatdan does this look good to go? I am going to take another syntax sweep today

LGTM
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

@vrothberg let me know if these package changes make sense, I put all of the parsing arguments in cmd/podman but put `CreateSCPCommand` in infra/abi since it is the only function used in both cmd and indea/abi. Keeping that function in cmd created an import error. I think the way I set this up makes sense!

actually... @vrothberg `CreateSCPCommand` will have to go into utils, referencing ABI in cmd or vice versa causes the cross tests to fail due to import of /libpod

EDIT: fixed the issue, put the function in utils/utils.go, I think this is necessary for compilation and makes the most sense as it is used by multiple files.
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL, I think this is good to go now after moving some functions around.
@vrothberg PTAL

Ok merging.  If people have issues, please open new ones. Want to get this into RC1
/lgtm
Nice job @cdoern
